what_it_does: Acceptance test for the speakable preprocessing layer: it verifies that brain replies are stripped of markdown, code fences, raw URLs, blockquotes, tables, emoji, and symbol runs, and that canonical expression tags are either mapped to engine-native syntax or stripped for unsupported TTS engines; it also includes a service-free wiring check that voice.loop.loop_turn cleans the reply before calling speak().
observations: Defines a global check(label, cond) helper that prints PASS/FAIL and increments a global PASS counter.
Tests markdown/code/URL/emoji stripping using a representative REPLY and the kokoro engine.
Tests engine-aware expression mapping: orpheus keeps <laugh>, chatterbox maps to [laugh], cosyvoice maps to [laughter], qwen3tts/xtts strip it.
Unknown engines strip expression markup and append a warning through an optional warn callback.
Fail-loud behavior: non-string input raises TypeError and a reply that cleans to empty raises ValueError.
Idempotence check: already-clean prose passes through unchanged.
Registry checks assert orpheus/chatterbox/cosyvoice are in ENGINE_EXPRESSION and all supported tags are in CANONICAL_TAGS.
Wiring section monkeypatches voice.loop.listen and voice.loop.speak to verify loop_turn cleans and maps the reply before TTS.
standouts: Pure-function acceptance suite that needs no TTS engine, bridge, or GPU.
Engine-specific expression syntax is treated as a registry-driven mapping, not a hard-coded per-test branch.
Fail-loud guards against silently synthesizing silence when a reply cleans to nothing.
Deterministic wiring test for the live voice loop path via monkeypatched service dependencies.
conventions: Uses sys.path.insert to locate the local company package before importing voice.speakable.
Module-level mutable state (PASS, ok) is updated by the check helper.
Tests print human-readable PASS/FAIL lines and exit with status 0 or 1.
Monkeypatched dependencies are restored in a try/finally block.
Docstring states the run command and explicitly flags what is and is not faked.
concerns: Global mutable PASS/ok state inside check makes the helper non-reentrant and harder to reuse.
Wiring test monkeypatches production module internals, coupling the test to voice.loop's function signatures.
Engine names, tag mappings, and the no-expression engine list are hard-coded rather than derived from configuration.
The assertion that kokoro output contains no 'laugh' substring is broad and could reject legitimate prose.
The test imports and depends on voice.loop internals, so it is not a pure unit test despite the docstring's framing.
notes: A service-free acceptance test that locks down the single speakable() transform and proves the voice loop calls it before TTS.
questions: Where is voice.speakable.speakable implemented and what is its exact strip/protect algorithm?
Does voice.loop.loop_turn already call speakable(), or is this test asserting the intended future wiring?
What is the complete set of CANONICAL_TAGS and ENGINE_EXPRESSION entries beyond the examples shown?
How are unknown-engine warnings surfaced in production outside the optional warn callback?
Are kokoro, qwen3tts, and xtts guaranteed to have no inline expression support, or is this an assumption?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file tests V-C stripping and V-D engine-aware expression mapping as a pure function, and adds a service-free wiring check via monkeypatch, exactly as the docstring describes.
apparent_themes: registry as single source of truth
intention_signals: Guarantee that every TTS-bound reply passes through one deterministic preprocessing step that removes text-model artifacts, adapts expression markup to each engine's capability, and never silently synthesizes silence, while also proving the voice loop actually invokes that step.
novelty: Tests the universal speakable transform as a pure function while also verifying live-loop wiring through monkeypatched STT/TTS dependencies, avoiding real engines entirely.
contribution: A deterministic acceptance suite that documents and guards the speakable preprocessing contract: markdown stripping, engine-specific expression tag handling, fail-loud behavior, and loop-turn wiring, without requiring TTS/STT services.
summary_for_embedding: Service-free acceptance test for the speakable() layer covering markdown/code/URL/emoji stripping, engine-specific expression-tag mapping, fail-loud behavior, and a monkeypatched wiring check for voice.loop.loop_turn.
intention_for_embedding: To prove that brain replies are cleaned and engine-mapped before reaching any TTS engine and that the voice loop invokes this preprocessing deterministically.

symbols.does: Test assertion helper that prints PASS or FAIL and increments the global PASS tally.
symbols.description: The check function evaluates a boolean condition, prints a labeled PASS/FAIL line, increments the module-level PASS counter on success, and sets the global ok flag to False on failure. It is the single assertion primitive used throughout the acceptance test.
